1: After this opened Job his mouth, and cursed his day.
3: There is a man child conceived.
4: Neither let the light shine upon it.
5: Let the blackness of the day terrify it.
6: Let it not come into the number of the months.
7: Let no joyful voice come therein.
8: Who are ready to raise up their mourning.
9: Neither let it see the dawning of the day:
10: Nor hid sorrow from mine eyes.
11: When I came out of the belly?
12: Or why the breasts that I should suck?
13: I should have slept: then had I been at rest,
14: Which built desolate places for themselves;
15: Who filled their houses with silver:
16: As infants which which never saw light.
17: And there the weary be at rest.
18: They hear not the voice of the oppressor.
19: And the servant is is free from his master.
20: And life unto the bitter in in soul;
21: And dig for it more than for hid treasures;
22: And And are glad, when they can find the grave?
23: And whom God hath hedged in?
24: And my roarings are poured out like the waters.
25: And that which I was afraid of is come unto me.
